headline: Jim Cramer Warns “I Don’t Want to Own AT&T or Verizon” as Starlink Threatens Telecom Stocks
summary: A Bernstein research note just slashed price targets across the entire telecom sector, and Jim Cramer is telling viewers to get out. The question dividing analysts is whether Starlink stays a rural curiosity or becomes a suburban threat that rewrites the competitive landscape for good.

content.title: Jim Cramer Warns “I Don’t Want to Own AT&T or Verizon” as Starlink Threatens Telecom Stocks
content.summary: A Bernstein research note just slashed price targets across the entire telecom sector, and Jim Cramer is telling viewers to get out. The question dividing analysts is whether Starlink stays a rural curiosity or becomes a suburban threat that rewrites the competitive landscape for good.

headline: Bernstein Slashes Targets On VZ, CHTR, T, CMCSA, TMUS Stocks: What’s The SpaceX Connection?
summary: Bernstein slashed the price targets on the five major telecom operators, citing valuation risks tied to SpaceX's plans to launch a direct-to-consumer Starlink mobile service.

headline: Verizon (VZ) Expands Into BMW Connected Cars And Launches Gizmo Watch 4
summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) is expanding its connected vehicle capabilities through a new partnership with BMW Group and KDDI, focused on 5G automotive services in the U.S. The company introduced the Gizmo Watch 4, a next generation kid focused wearable that adds new safety features and family oriented rewards incentives. Verizon was identified as a key participant in the high growth Unified Communication as a Service (UCaaS) market, tied to demand for cloud based collaboration and...

headline: A Dividend Portfolio That Can Cover the Cost of Living in San Francisco
summary: San Francisco makes passive income math unforgiving. SmartAsset’s 2026 comfort-salary study estimates that a single adult needs about $134,950 in pretax income to live comfortably in the city, among the highest figures in the country. Turning that paycheck into dividend income is not just a yield exercise. The yield an investor reaches for changes both ... A Dividend Portfolio That Can Cover the Cost of Living in San Francisco

headline: Zayo Announces Planned CEO Transition as Company Enters Next Phase of AI-Driven Digital Infrastructure Growth
summary: DENVER, July 14, 2026--Zayo ("the Company"), the leading digital infrastructure network provider, today announced a planned CEO transition, appointing former Verizon executive Sowmyanarayan Sampath as Chief Executive Officer (CEO), effective September 1, 2026. Sampath will succeed Steve Smith, who will retire as CEO following a transformative tenure that established Zayo as the foundational backbone of the AI economy. Smith will continue to serve as a member of Zayo’s Board of Directors.

content.title: Zayo Announces Planned CEO Transition as Company Enters Next Phase of AI-Driven Digital Infrastructure Growth
content.summary: DENVER, July 14, 2026--Zayo ("the Company"), the leading digital infrastructure network provider, today announced a planned CEO transition, appointing former Verizon executive Sowmyanarayan Sampath as Chief Executive Officer (CEO), effective September 1, 2026. Sampath will succeed Steve Smith, who will retire as CEO following a transformative tenure that established Zayo as the foundational backbone of the AI economy. Smith will continue to serve as a member of Zayo’s Board of Directors.

headline: 3 Dividend Stocks That Pay More Than Social Security COLA
summary: Social Security's 2026 cost-of-living adjustment barely keeps pace with a grocery bill, but three dividend stocks have been outpacing it for decades with yields that range from nearly 4% to almost 7%.

content.title: 3 Dividend Stocks That Pay More Than Social Security COLA

headline: T-Mobile Will Survive Threat from SpaceX’s Starlink and Is Best Bet in Wireless
summary: SpaceX  shares fell back to Earth following their blockbuster initial public offering.  T-Mobile has fallen slightly since SpaceX’s IPO in mid-June and is down 8% this year, partly due to worries about competition from Starlink.  Shares of  Verizon  and  AT&T  as well as cable companies  Comcast  and  Charter Communications  have all slid since SpaceX went public.

content.title: T-Mobile Will Survive Threat from SpaceX’s Starlink and Is Best Bet in Wireless
